metadata.description: Comprehensive Eiken Grade 5 vocabulary for upper elementary school students (ages 10-12)

examples: I do my homework every day.
What do you do on Sundays?
She does yoga in the morning.
I go to school by bus.
We go to the park on weekends.
He goes to bed at 9 o'clock.
My friends come to my house.
I think it will rain today.
She thinks this is a good idea.
What do you think about this?
He drinks water when he is thirsty.
I play soccer with my friends.
We play games in the park.
We watch the stars at night.
We read many stories at school.
She writes letters to her friends.
We study hard for the test.
My father works in an office.
We play jump rope at recess.
The baby sleeps in the afternoon.
We take the bus to school.
I give a gift to my friend.
She gives food to the cat.
I learn new things every day.
She learns to play the guitar.
I cook dinner for my family.
I buy books at the shop.
We buy food at the supermarket.
I see a bird in the tree.
She sees her friends at school.
We see many stars at night.
I meet my friends at school.
I ask the teacher a question.
I tell my mother about school.
She cries when she is sad.
She hates going to bed early.
I wear a uniform to school.
We wear warm clothes in winter.
I put my book on the desk.
She puts the bag on the chair.
We put the plates on the table.
We stop at the red light.
I move to a new city.
I hope to see you soon.
She is tall for her age.
We move to a new house.
She is scared of the dark.
My heart is full of joy.
She goes to the hospital when sick.
Practice makes you strong, not weak.
Nothing is boring if you try.
We buy cheap things, not expensive.
I like cheap but good things.
We are in the same class.
I often go to the park.
We go on a trip tomorrow.
We stay inside on rainy days.
We look at the photo sadly.
We are too tired to play.
He tells stories to his grandfather.
We see many children in the park.
I have many friends at school.
She wants to be a teacher.
She wants to be a doctor.
We go to the doctor when sick.
She has a brother, a boy.
She sees a man in the park.
A man walks down the street.
I go to school every day.
We take notes in the notebook.
The teacher writes on the blackboard.
I do well on the test.
We study hard for good grades.
We learn about nature in science.
We listen to music in music class.
She lives in a big house.
Home is where we feel safe.
We watch TV in the living room.
She sits in the living room.
My mother cooks in the kitchen.
I take a shower in the bathroom.
The table is made of wood.
The shower is in the bathroom.
I brush my teeth with a toothbrush.
I use a towel after a shower.
She checks her hair in the mirror.
I put clothes in the basket.
We lock the door with a key.
I put my toys in the box.
I eat soup with a spoon.
She buys food at the shop.
I have three meals a day.
I eat an egg for breakfast.
I eat cake on my birthday.
We have ice cream in summer.
We have tea in the afternoon.
We buy juice at the shop.
She sees animals at the zoo.
She keeps fish in a tank.
I see a lion at the zoo.
We see birds in the sky.
She stays inside during a storm.
We see lightning in the sky.
She cannot see in the fog.
I wake up in the morning.
I have lunch in the afternoon.
I eat dinner in the evening.
She watches TV in the evening.
There are 7 days in a week.
There are 12 months in a year.
We wear warm clothes in winter.
She visits family on New Year.
I live in a small town.
She plants flowers in the garden.
We buy things at the shop.
She buys food at the market.
I buy food at the supermarket.
She saves money at the bank.
The bank is near my house.
I go to the post office.
She sends letters at the post office.
The post office is near school.
She works at the police station.
Firefighters work at the fire station.
The fire station helps put out fires.
She meets me at the station.
We drink tea at the cafe.
We watch movies at the cinema.
We find our way with a map.
We take care of our body.
We carry bags on our shoulders.
I have pain in my chest.
We digest food in our stomach.
I have pain in my leg.
A mountain looks like a triangle.
I wear a coat in winter.
We find things on the internet.
She goes in a new direction.
We are on the same side.
She goes to the city center.
The book is on the desk.
The cat is under the table.
The bird flies over the tree.
The sun is above the clouds.
The fish is below the water.
She buys a gift for mom.
I sit between Tom and Mary.
I like tea but not coffee.
Do you like tea or coffee?
She goes by bus or train.
I am happy because it's sunny.
She sleeps late because she works.
It's raining so I stay home.
She is sick so she rests.
I eat when I am hungry.
She sleeps when she is tired.
I will go if it's sunny.
She studies if she has a test.
We play if we have time.
Although it's cold, I go out.
She studies although she is tired.
We go to school every day.
Excuse me, where is the station?
We say sorry when we make mistakes.
